entries.name: A 2nd Pistol
entries.description: A heavy, single-shot, breech-loading firearm. Devastating at 20 paces, slow to reload.

entries.name: Concealed palm pistol
entries.description:
A small firearm with a weak charge, easily concealed in a sleeve or waistcoat.

This pistol has extremely limited range; only a few feet. It’s very difficult to detect on your person, even if you’re searched.

entries.name: A Large Weapon
entries.description: A weapon meant for two hands. A battle-axe, greatsword, warhammer, or pole-arm. A hunting rifle. A blunderbuss. A bow or crossbow.

entries.name: Fine shadow cloak
entries.description: A hooded cloak made of rare Iruvian shadow-silk thatblends into the darkness around it. This item improves your effect level whenyou sneak around.

entries.name: Armor + Heavy
entries.description: The addition of chain mail, metal plates, a metal helm.

entries.name: Fine hand weapon
entries.description:
A finely crafted one-handed melee weapon of your choice.

Is this a well-crafted standard weapon, like a perfectly-balanced dagger, or something exotic, like an Iruvian dueling saber or a metal-banded war-club?

entries.name: Fine disguise kit
entries.description:
A theatrical make-up kit equipped with an impressive array of expert appliances to fool the eye.

The fine quality of this kit may increase the effect of your deceptive actions when you use it.

entries.name: Blowgun & darts, syringes
entries.description: A small tube and darts that can be filled from alchemy flasks. Empty syringes.

entries.name: Spirit bottles (2)
entries.description: An arcane device used to trap a spirit. A metal and crystalline cylinder, the size of a loaf of bread.

entries.name: Spiritbane Charm
entries.description: A small arcane trinket that ghosts prefer to avoid.

entries.name: A trained hunting pet
entries.description: Your animal companion obeys your commands and anticipates your actions. Cohort (Expert: Hunter).

entries.name: Fine heavy weapon
entries.description:
A finely crafted two-handed melee weapon of your choice.

A warhammer, a greatsword, a military pike, a battleaxe, etc. A heavy weapon has more reach and hits harder than a standard weapon. This might give you potency when the power or reach of the weapon is a factor.

entries.name: Trance powder
entries.description:
A dose of the popular drug, which induces an altered mental state.

The victim of this powder is not fully unconscious, but rather retreats into a calm, suggestible mental state, similar to hypnotism.

entries.name: An Unusual Weapon
entries.description: A curiosity or tool turned into a weapon. A whip, a flail, a hatchet, a shovel, a length of chain, a razor-edged fan, steel-toed boots.

entries.name: Vial of slumber essence
entries.description:
A dose of slumber essence sufficient to put someone to sleep for an hour.

The victim’s sleep isn’t supernatural, but it is deep—they can be roused with some effort.

entries.name: Silence potion vial
entries.description: A vial of golden liquid that negates all sound within 10 paces of the drinker for a span of several moments.

entries.name: A Blade or Two
entries.description: Perhaps you carry a simple fighting knife. Or two curved swords. Or a rapier and stiletto. Or a heavy butcher’s cleaver.

entries.name: Tinkering Tools
entries.description: An assortment for detailed mechanist work: jeweler’s loupe, tweezers, a small hammer, pliers, screwdriver, etc.

entries.name: Bandolier (3 uses)
entries.description:
A strap worn across the body, fitted with specially-padded pouches to hold three alchemical agents or spark-craft bombs. When you employ an alchemical or bomb from a bandolier, choose one from the list at right (or one of your custom-made formulas). See page 226 for more on alchemicals and bombs.

During downtime, you automatically refill your bandoliers, so long as you have reasonable access to a supplier or workshop.

  • Alcahest
  • Binding Oil
  • Drift Oil
  • Drown Powder
  • Eyeblind Poison
  • Fire Oil
  • Grenade
  • Quicksilver
  • Skullfire Poison
  • Smoke Bomb
  • Spark (drug)
  • Standstill Poison
  • Trance Powder

entries.name: Fine spirit mask
entries.description: An arcane item that allows the trained user to see supernatural energies in great detail. Also affords some measure of protection against ghostly possession. Each spirit mask is unique. What does yours look like? What makes it strange and disturbing to see?

entries.name: Electroplasmic ammunition
entries.description: A bandolier of electroplasmic ammo, especially potent against spirits, but less effective against physical targets. The electrical charge is enough to stun a person, but does very little real harm. Several hits might incapacitate a human target. This ammunition is especially reactive in the ghost field—make a 4-clock called “Attention from the Spirit Wardens” and tick it for every operation in which this ammo was used.

entries.name: Fine loaded dice, trick cards
entries.description:
Gambling accouterments subtly altered to favor particular outcomes.

The fine quality of this kit may increase the effect of your deceptive actions when you use it.

entries.name: Documents
entries.description: A collection of slim volumes on a variety of topics, including a registry of the nobility, City Watch commanders, and other notable citizens. Blank pages, a vial of ink, a pen. A number of interesting maps.

entries.name: Gadgets
entries.description:
You may create gadgets during downtime by Tinkering with tools and materials. See Gadgets, page 227. Track the load for each gadget you deploy during an operation.

<You can create element for each Gadget and add to character sheet to track them, each of them has 1 load>

entries.name: Demolition Tools
entries.description: A sledgehammer and iron spikes. Heavy drill. Crowbar.

entries.name: Fine cover identity
entries.description: Paperwork, planted stories and rumors, and false relationships sufficient to pass as a different person.

entries.name: Manacles & chain
entries.description:
A set of heavy manacles and chain, suitable for restraining a prisoner.

A souvenir from a stay with the Bluecoats, perhaps?

entries.name: Throwing Knives
entries.description: Six small, light blades.

entries.name: Spyglass
entries.description: A brass tube with lenses that allow long-distance vision. Collapsible. May attach to a rifle.

entries.name: Fine lightning hook
entries.description: A long, two-handed pole with a loop of heavy wire at the end, connected to an electroplasmic capacitor. Suitable for grappling a spirit and dragging it into a spirit bottle. This custom-made hook collapses into a compact form, thus reducing its load to 1, even though it’s two-handed.

entries.name: Light climbing gear
entries.description:
A well-crafted set of climbing gear that is less bulky and heavy than a standard set.

Standard climbing gear is 2 load.

entries.name: Rage essence vial
entries.description:
A single dose, which greatly enhances the user’s strength, resistance to pain, and irrational aggression for the span of several minutes.

The GM will modify your position and effect accordingly when you fight on rage essence. Also, you suffer two consequences: “Can’t Tell Friend From Foe” and “Can’t Stop Until They’re All Broken.” You may resist these as usual.

entries.name: Lantern
entries.description: A simple oil lantern, a fancy electroplasmic lamp, or other light source.

entries.name: A Pistol
entries.description: A heavy, single-shot, breech-loading firearm. Devastating at 20 paces, slow to reload.

entries.name: Fine long rifle
entries.description: A finely crafted hunting rifle, deadly at long range, unwieldy in close quarters. Long rifles are usually illegal for private citizens in Doskvol, but you have (real or forged) military paperwork for this one.

entries.name: Fine pair of pistols
entries.description: A matched pair of handguns, made for greater accuracy, with double barrels that allow for two shots before reloading. Were your pistols made by Kardera’s Daughters, Templeton & Slane, the Imperial Forge, or some other gunsmith? How do they stand out from the average handgun?

entries.name: Climbing Gear
entries.description: A large coil of rope. A small coil of rope. Grappling hooks. A small pouch of chalk dust. A climbing harness with loops and metal rings. A set of iron pitons and a small mallet.

entries.name: Blueprints
entries.description:
A folio of useful architectural drawings and city plans.

Feel free to specify which plans you’re carrying when you choose this item.

entries.name: Dark-sight goggles
entries.description: An arcane device that allows the wearer to see in pitch darkness as if it were well-lit.

entries.name: Scary weapon or tool
entries.description: A scary-looking hand weapon or tool. This item grants increased effect when you intimidate, not increased harm in combat.

entries.name: Arcane Implements
entries.description: A vial of quicksilver. A pouch of black salt. A spirit anchor in the form of a small stone. A spirit bottle. A vial of electroplasm, designed to break and splatter on impact.

entries.name: Fine bottle of whiskey
entries.description: A rare distillation from your personal collection, potent both in its alcohol and its ability to impress.

entries.name: Fine clothes & jewelry
entries.description:
An outfit that appears to be of such fine make as to pass you off as a wealthy noble.

If you’re carrying this item as a second outfit to change into, it counts as 2 load.

entries.name: A cane-sword
entries.description:
A slim sword and its sheath, disguised as a noble’s cane.

The disguise will fool a cursory inspection.

entries.name: Demonbane charm
entries.description: An arcane trinket that demons prefer to avoid.

entries.name: Subterfuge Supplies
entries.description: A theatrical make- up kit. A selection of blank documents, ready for the forger’s hand. Costume jewelry. A reversible cloak and distinctive hat. A forged badge of office.

entries.name: Fine wrecking tools
entries.description: A specialized set of tools for sabotage and destruction. A small, powerful drill. A mallet and steel spikes. A prybar. An electroplasmic battery, clamps, wire. Vials of acid. A spark-torch cutter and fuel tank.

entries.name: Fine lockpicks
entries.description: A finely crafted set of tools to disable and circumvent locks.

entries.name: Fine tickering tools
entries.description: A finely crafted set of tools for detailed mechanist work. A jeweler’s loupe. Measuring devices.

entries.name: Bandolier (3 uses)
entries.description:
A strap worn across the body, fitted with specially-padded pouches to hold three alchemical agents or spark-craft bombs. When you employ an alchemical or bomb from a bandolier, choose one from the list at right (or one of your custom-made formulas). See page 226 for more on alchemicals and bombs.

During downtime, you automatically refill your bandoliers, so long as you have reasonable access to a supplier or workshop.

  • Alcahest
  • Binding Oil
  • Drift Oil
  • Drown Powder
  • Eyeblind Poison
  • Fire Oil
  • Grenade
  • Quicksilver
  • Skullfire Poison
  • Smoke Bomb
  • Spark (drug)
  • Standstill Poison
  • Trance Powder

entries.name: Armor
entries.description: A thick leather tunic plus reinforced gloves and boots.

entries.name: Burglary Gear
entries.description: A set of lockpicks. A small pry-bar. Vials of oil to silence squeaky hinges. A coil of wire and fishing hooks. A small pouch of fine sand.

entries.name: Ghost key
entries.description: An arcane device that can open ghost doors. There’s an echo of the entire city, across the ages, trapped in the ghost field. Sometimes a door to that place can be found.
